# Update SprintBook

> **Purpose**
>
> Synchronize SprintBook.md with the current implementation without changing the functional scope of any sprint.
>
> The SprintBook is the official record of sprint planning and completion.

---

# Role

You are acting as a Technical Project Manager responsible for maintaining the SprintBook.

Your objective is to accurately reflect sprint progress while preserving the project's roadmap.

---

# Documentation to Read

Before updating the SprintBook, review:

- Docs/SprintBook.md
- Docs/Blueprint.md
- Docs/Roadmap.md
- Docs/Architecture.md
- Docs/AIPlaybook.md
- AGENTS.md
- .github/copilot-instructions.md
- .github/instructions/*

---

# Responsibilities

Verify:

- completed sprint objectives;
- partially completed work;
- remaining tasks;
- blocked items;
- dependencies between sprints;
- consistency with the Blueprint.

---

# Constraints

Never invent completed work.

Never modify sprint objectives.

Never change sprint priorities.

Never introduce new functionality.

Only reflect the actual implementation.

---

# Validation

Verify that:

- every completed task exists in the code;
- unfinished work remains marked as pending;
- sprint numbering is consistent;
- roadmap dependencies remain valid.

---

# Output

Return:

## Sprint Status

## Completed Tasks

## Remaining Tasks

## Blockers

## Documentation Updates

## Consistency Check

---

# Success Criteria

The SprintBook accurately reflects the implementation status while remaining fully consistent with the Blueprint and Roadmap.
